title: Pouteria surumuensis
intro: Pouteria surumuensis, commonly known as Surumu tree, is a tropical plant species belonging to the family Sapotaceae, native to the Amazon rainforest. The bark and roots of this plant have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Pouteria surumuensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. In traditional medicine, the Surumu tree is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues and boost the immune system.
taxonomy: Pouteria surumuensis is classified in the family Sapotaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as sapotes. This family is part of the larger order Ericales, which includes a wide variety of plant species. The genus Pouteria itself is native to the tropical regions of the Americas and contains several species of trees and shrubs. Pouteria surumuensis is a species within this genus and is native to the country of Brazil. Its classification reflects its evolutionary relationship with other plants in the Sapotaceae family.
